skill.name: rebuttal-letter-strategist
skill.description: Generate professional rebuttal responses to peer review criticism for academic publishing.
skill.summary: Draft strategic, evidence-based responses to reviewer comments for journal submissions.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: A legitimate academic writing skill for drafting peer review rebuttal letters. All 44 static analysis findings were evaluated as false positives: weak cryptographic algorithm hits match argparse usage and documentation text (not cryptography); path traversal hits match a security checklist documenting what to prevent; system reconnaissance hits match audit result JSON text and markdown documentation; and external command hits match commands inside markdown code blocks (documentation examples). No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected. The skill is a well-documented, safe academic tool.

content.user_title: Craft Strategic Rebuttal Letters for Peer Review
content.value_statement: Academic researchers struggle to respond to harsh or critical peer review comments effectively. This skill helps draft professional, evidence-based rebuttal responses that address reviewer concerns while maintaining a diplomatic tone.
actual_capabilities: Analyze reviewer criticism to determine appropriate response severity from minor to harsh
Draft professional rebuttal responses using predefined templates for each severity level
Generate strategic tone and approach recommendations for responding to peer review
Integrate specific evidence and revision details into structured rebuttal responses
Support point-by-point responses to multiple reviewer comments in a consistent tone
Provide a documented workflow with explicit assumptions, inputs, and output boundaries
limitations: Does not analyze or modify the underlying research manuscript text
Cannot guarantee reviewer or editor acceptance of the rebuttal response
Does not review the scientific accuracy or validity of rebuttal content
Requires user-provided criticism text and revision details as input

use_cases.title: Major Revision Response
use_cases.description: Draft diplomatic responses to major revision requests with detailed evidence-based explanations addressing reviewer concerns.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate students responding to their first major journal revision

use_cases.title: Rejection Appeal Letter
use_cases.description: Craft professional appeals for rejected manuscripts by addressing reviewer concerns systematically and respectfully.
use_cases.target_user: Experienced researchers appealing journal rejection decisions

use_cases.title: Point-by-Point Rebuttal
use_cases.description: Generate structured responses to multiple reviewer comments maintaining a consistent professional tone across all replies.
use_cases.target_user: Postdoctoral researchers preparing comprehensive revision response packages

prompt_templates.title: Accept a minor suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: The reviewer said: 'The introduction could be more concise to better engage readers.' We shortened the introduction by 20 percent and added a clear thesis statement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple acceptance of a minor revision suggestion with brief acknowledgment

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a moderate concern
prompt_templates.prompt: The reviewer said: 'The methodology section is unclear and lacks sufficient detail for reproducibility.' We added more detail to the methodology section and moved detailed protocols to the supplementary materials.
prompt_templates.scenario: Addressing a moderate concern with a clarification and reorganization in the revised manuscript

prompt_templates.title: Address a major revision with evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: The reviewer said: 'The sample size is too small to draw meaningful conclusions from this data.' We performed a post-hoc power analysis that confirmed adequate statistical power. We also added this analysis to the results section.
prompt_templates.scenario: Responding to a major methodological concern with supporting quantitative evidence

prompt_templates.title: De-escalate harsh criticism
prompt_templates.prompt: The reviewer said: 'This work is fundamentally flawed and adds nothing new to the field.' We respectfully disagree and have strengthened our argument by adding two new control experiments and a more thorough discussion of limitations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Professional de-escalation of a harsh review while maintaining scientific integrity

output_examples.input:
Criticism: 'The statistical analysis is inadequate for the study design.'
Response: 'We added a mixed-effects model analysis to account for repeated measures.'
output_examples.output:
REBUTTAL STRATEGY
Criticism severity: MAJOR
Recommended tone: diplomatic
Strategy: Address with evidence
Suggested response: We respectfully address this concern by adding a mixed-effects model analysis to account for repeated measures.

output_examples.input:
Criticism: 'The discussion section repeats the results without interpretation.'
Response: 'We restructured the discussion to focus on implications and future directions.'
output_examples.output:
REBUTTAL STRATEGY
Criticism severity: MODERATE
Recommended tone: constructive
Strategy: Acknowledge and clarify
Suggested response: We appreciate this important point. We have now clarified this in the revised manuscript.

output_examples.input:
Criticism: 'Figure 3 labels are difficult to read in print format.'
Response: 'We increased font size and improved contrast in Figure 3.'
output_examples.output:
REBUTTAL STRATEGY
Criticism severity: MINOR
Recommended tone: appreciative
Strategy: Accept and thank
Suggested response: We thank the reviewer for this insightful comment. We have revised the manuscript accordingly.

best_practices: Always include specific evidence or revision details in your response to demonstrate concrete action
Match your response tone to the severity level of the criticism for appropriate professional positioning
Keep responses professional and avoid defensive or confrontational language even when criticism is harsh
anti_patterns: Do not ignore or dismiss reviewer concerns without providing evidence or clear reasoning
Do not use aggressive, sarcastic, or confrontational language in rebuttal responses
Do not fabricate data, citations, or changes that were not actually made to the manuscript

faq.question: What types of criticism can Rebuttal Letter Strategist handle?
faq.answer: The tool analyzes criticism and categorizes it into four levels: minor, moderate, major, and harsh. Each level has a specific response strategy and recommended tone.

faq.question: Does this tool guarantee acceptance of my manuscript?
faq.answer: No. The tool helps draft professional responses, but it cannot guarantee reviewer or editor acceptance. The quality of the rebuttal still depends on your specific evidence and revisions.

faq.question: Can I respond to multiple reviewer comments at once?
faq.answer: Yes. You can use the tool multiple times for different comments. Each response is generated independently based on the specific criticism and revision details you provide.

faq.question: Do I need to install Python to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The Python script is a reference implementation. When using this skill with Claude or Codex, the AI can generate rebuttal responses without running the script directly.

faq.question: Can I customize the response templates?
faq.answer: Yes. The predefined templates use placeholder text that you can replace with specific revision details. This allows you to tailor responses to your particular situation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for any academic field?
faq.answer: Yes. The tool generates general professional rebuttal language suitable for any academic discipline. You can provide field-specific language through the revision description.
